behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_CUSION::look' (examine) event for the cushion item. When triggered it establishes an info/context on a referenced object (push 0x208 + indirect object) and excludes the current process from that context, constructs a temporary string "cushion ", calls Item::bark to present that text to the player/viewer, frees the temporary string, and then uses the bark return value in a short implies/pop sequence before suspending/returning.
notable_control_flow: Entry via Event 0 (U8P_CUSION::look) — short, single-purpose handler.
Sets a contextual info key (0x208) on an object and issues a process-exclude operation — a guard or presentation-context setup before speaking.
Allocates/creates a temporary heap string ('cushion '), passes it to Item::bark, then frees the string (cleanup).
Captures Item::bark return value and immediately uses it in an implies/pop pattern prior to suspend/return (likely to propagate or handle the bark result).
open_questions: What exactly does the info id 0x208 represent in engine semantics (presentation context, logged metadata, or other)? (inferred)
Which PID is passed first to Item::bark (the item's PID as 'this' vs. the viewer/process PID) — parameter order is inferred from call pattern but not explicit.
What precise effect does the implies/pop sequence have here (flow control, return normalization, or interruption handling)?

main_function.name: U8P_CUSION::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a player/actor examining the cushion by presenting a short descriptive bark ('cushion ') and cleaning up temporary resources after establishing a presentation/info context.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — 'U8P_CUSION::look' (triggered when an actor/player examines the cushion item)

function_map.name: U8P_CUSION::look
function_map.role: Event handler for the cushion object's 'U8P_CUSION::look' (examine) action; prepares context, invokes Item::bark with the text 'cushion ', and performs cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API called to present/display short text or message originating from an item/process.

external_calls.purpose: Cause the cushion item to 'bark' / display the short descriptive string "cushion " to the appropriate process/player.
Free the temporary heap string created to hold the literal 'cushion ' after it has been passed to Item::bark.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_CUSION::look' (examine) event for the cushion item. When triggered it establishes an info/context on a referenced object (push 0x208 + indirect object) and excludes the current process from that context, constructs a temporary string "cushion ", calls Item::bark to present that text to the player/viewer, frees the temporary string, and then uses the bark return value in a short implies/pop sequence before suspending/returning.
